summary: The diff implements a campus complaint management system using an Internet Computer (ICP/Motoko) blockchain backend with Internet Identity authentication, rather than the specified Express/SQLite/username-password/Nodemailer/Socket.io stack. Many core requirements are partially or fully missing, and several constraint violations are present (wrong auth, wrong backend, wrong DB, etc.).

missing_requirements.requirement: Username/password authentication with four roles stored in SQLite. Persistent login via localStorage. Sessions survive page refresh until explicit logout.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff uses Internet Identity (ICP blockchain auth via @dfinity/auth-client), not username/password. There is no registration form, no password hashing, and no localStorage session — the constraint explicitly forbids Google OAuth and third-party auth, and the build request requires username/password only.

missing_requirements.requirement: Email notifications via Nodemailer with environment-configured SMTP credentials when complaint status changes, on registration, and on resolution.
missing_requirements.reason: No Nodemailer integration or backend email sending is present anywhere in the diff. The backend is Motoko on ICP, not Express, so SMTP email is not implemented.

missing_requirements.requirement: Real-time emergency alerts broadcast via Socket.io WebSockets to Admin and relevant HOD.
missing_requirements.reason: No Socket.io integration exists. Emergency alerts are simulated locally in frontend state without real WebSocket broadcasting to other connected clients.

missing_requirements.requirement: SQLite database with all seven specified tables (Users, Departments, Complaints, Complaint_Media, Notifications, Feedback, Emergencies), seeded departments, cascading deletion, and unique complaint ID generation on the backend.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend is Motoko on Internet Computer using in-memory Maps, not SQLite. None of the seven specified tables exist. Department seeding, cascading deletion, and the complete schema are absent.

missing_requirements.requirement: All backend logic must remain in a single Express server file.
missing_requirements.reason: There is no Express server file at all. The backend is an ICP Motoko actor, violating the constraint.

missing_requirements.requirement: Media (photos, videos, voice notes) stored in the SQLite database as binary/base64 data, not on an external file system.
missing_requirements.reason: Media is stored in frontend localStorage/state (localComplaintStore) as base64 data URLs, not persisted to any backend database. The constraint requires SQLite storage.

missing_requirements.requirement: Complaint category, department, priority, deadline, and anonymous flag persisted in the backend database.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend Complaint type only stores id, studentId, description, status, priority, and createdAt. Category, department, deadline, and is_anonymous are not in the backend schema and appear stored only in frontend localComplaintStore.

missing_requirements.requirement: PDF-format report generation for complaint history.
missing_requirements.reason: No PDF generation library or export functionality is visible in any of the diff files provided.

missing_requirements.requirement: Admin can bulk purge all accounts of a specific role and delete individual user accounts with cascading effect.
missing_requirements.reason: No user deletion or bulk purge endpoint exists in the Motoko backend.
No escalation functionality is present in the backend or visible frontend pages.

missing_requirements.requirement: Admin can run AI predictive analysis from the dashboard.
missing_requirements.reason: No AI predictive analysis panel or trigger is evident from the diff files provided.

missing_requirements.requirement: Feedback and ratings persisted in backend database and visible to Admin and HOD.
missing_requirements.reason: Feedback is stored only in frontend localComplaintStore (localStorage), not in any backend database. There is no Feedback table in the Motoko backend.

missing_requirements.requirement: Only the HOD of the student's department receives the emergency overlay, not other HODs. Emergency routed server-side to correct HOD via Socket.io.
missing_requirements.reason: There is no real-time Socket.io server. The emergency overlay is triggered locally in the frontend only for the current user if they happen to be HOD/Admin, with no actual cross-client routing to the correct department HOD.

missing_requirements.requirement: Emergency events logged in the database with timestamp and student info.
missing_requirements.reason: Emergency events are stored only in localStorage via localComplaintStore, not in any backend database.

missing_requirements.requirement: Delete Account button permanently deletes user account and all associated data from the backend database, then clears session and redirects.
missing_requirements.reason: No delete account endpoint exists in the Motoko backend. The sidebar shows a delete option but backend cascading deletion is not implemented.

missing_requirements.requirement: On app startup, verify stored session user still exists in the database; if not, auto-clear ghost session and redirect to login.
missing_requirements.reason: The app uses Internet Identity delegation validity checks, not a database user existence check. There is no mechanism to verify if a username/password account still exists in SQLite.

missing_requirements.requirement: Anonymous complaint identity hidden server-side from Staff/HOD but revealed to Admin via backend query.
missing_requirements.reason: The is_anonymous flag is stored only in frontend localComplaintStore. The backend Motoko complaint type has no is_anonymous field, so server-side enforcement is absent.

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity (ICP blockchain authentication) used instead of username/password authentication.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request explicitly prohibits Google OAuth or any third-party authentication and mandates username/password only. Internet Identity is a third-party cryptographic auth system not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Motoko actor on Internet Computer used as the backend instead of a single Express.js server file with SQLite.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request requires all backend logic in a single Express server file with SQLite. The diff uses a completely different technology stack (Motoko/ICP).

hallucinated_features.description: MixinAuthorization and AccessControl modules imported from ICP-specific authorization packages.
hallucinated_features.reason: These are ICP-platform-specific modules not requested in the build specification.

hallucinated_features.description: Complaints and user data stored in localStorage (localComplaintStore) on the frontend as the primary persistence layer.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request requires all data persisted in a SQLite backend database. Using localStorage as the primary data store was not requested and violates data persistence requirements.

hallucinated_features.description: ProfileSetupModal for first-time setup after Internet Identity login instead of a registration form with username/password.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request requires a registration form supporting all four roles with username/password, not a post-login profile setup modal.

hallucinated_features.description: TanStack Query (React Query) with ICP actor pattern used for all data fetching.
hallucinated_features.reason: This is an implementation detail tied to the ICP stack and not requested. The build request specifies a REST API from an Express server.
